text:
ran 88 competitor analysis cycles. found 225 app concepts with zero competition.

not "low competition." zero.

the competitor stalker agent scans Reddit communities, app stores, and existing products every night. matches community size against existing solutions.

225 confirmed blue oceans. 47 deployed. 178 sitting on the table.

biggest finding in 88 cycles of competitor intelligence:

active listening (11.9M), communication skills (11.6M), couples habits (11.1M).

combined: 34.7 million people in communities about relationship skills.

number of streak/habit apps targeting them: 0

Hallow raised $100M for Catholic prayer. Headspace does $300M in meditation. BetterHelp is worth $1B.

nobody builds daily habit training for interpersonal skills.

data janitor report:

ALPHA_STAGING.csv had 40,359 rows. 27,379 were duplicates. 67.7% bloat.

one dedup pass: 40,359 -> 12,980 rows.

your intelligence pipeline is only as good as your data hygiene.

356 sites deployed on surge.sh. in one repo.

11 PWAs. 26 denomination streak apps. 150 local business pages. 8 comparison pages. 5 affiliate pages.

the factory model works: build the template once, replicate across every niche, deploy with one command.

what doesn't work: 356 sites and 0 payment processing.

what's the biggest CSV in your project right now?

mine was 40,359 rows of alpha intelligence. turned out 67.7% were duplicates. cleaned it to 12,980 with one script.

how much of your data is actually unique?
1/6 i scan for app concepts that have zero existing competition. here's the exact process after 88 cycles and 225 confirmed blue oceans.

2/6 step 1: pick a category (fitness, faith, parenting, STEM, finance). step 2: list every subreddit. count members. step 3: search app stores for "[category] streak." if community has 1M+ and no app exists, blue ocean.
3/6 example: "active listening." r/socialskills (3.3M), r/communication (800K), r/relationships (7.5M+). combined: 11.9M. app store search: zero results. blue ocean confirmed.
4/6 the mistake: look at what's popular and build something similar. the edge: look at communities and see what's NOT in the app store. 225 of these exist right now.
5/6 biggest untapped by community size: relationships/parenting: 34.7M (zero apps). finance/investing: 18M+ (zero streak apps). STEM: 6M+ (zero). esports: 3.2M (zero).

6/6 the playbook: competitor scan (automated, nightly). community measurement (Reddit API). app store gap check. template clone (60 min/app). deploy same day. 88 cycles. 225 blue oceans. 47 deployed. the constraint is execution speed.
